description: A bowl remains 'whoa' after a fall if its parts are present and its form is intact. The adjective can also describe a body or community considered with all relevant members included. 'sholu' (complete) says that an expected set or process has everything required; 'whoa' says the thing itself holds together as one.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiceless 'wh'. Air passes without vocal-fold vibration into true-mid back 'o'. Keep the following central 'a' in a separate syllable by opening the jaw and releasing the lip rounding after the hiatus. Stress falls on 'who'.
sound_symbolism: The rounded voiceless opening gathers the breath into one shape, then the separate 'a' opens it without breaking the contour. That motion recalls an intact thing making room around all its parts.
usage_notes: The adjective can stand as the quality noun wholeness. When the relevant boundary is not obvious, context supplies it: a system may be whole for one analysis while belonging to a larger whole in another.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Regenerative work often asks whether a repair restores relations around the damaged part. 'whoa' can name the resulting wholeness once the boundary and the included parts are clear.

semantic_domains.physical: an object or body whose relevant parts are present and joined
semantic_domains.quantity: all relevant parts included in the total under discussion
